summary: SKBitmap.Decode and SKCodec.Create appear to hang indefinitely when decoding a PNG containing a ~100MB Exif metadata chunk, because Skia's PNG codec reads all pre-IDAT chunks in 4KB increments with no size limit or cancellation mechanism.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Code execution is blocked on SKBitmap.Decode method

stepsToReproduce: Use Magick.NET to generate a 512x512 PNG with a ~100MB Exif UserComment
Call SKCodec.Create(stream) or SKBitmap.Decode(stream) on the resulting stream
Observe that the call blocks indefinitely (actually processing very slowly)
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 2.80.2, .NET 5 Console App, Rider JetBrains IDE
codeSnippets:
var skCodec = SKCodec.Create(stream);
var skBitmap = SKBitmap.Decode(skCodec);

repoLinks.description: PR #1518: Add support for reading Exif SubIFD (merged 2020-10-12)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The PNG chunk processing code path in SkPngCodec::decodeBounds() has not changed — it still reads all pre-IDAT chunks in 4KB increments with no size limit.

analysis.summary: The PNG codec in Skia reads every chunk before IDAT during codec creation. A ~100MB metadata chunk is processed in 4KB increments via process_data(), making it appear hung. There is no size limit, timeout, or cancellation mechanism at the native level. This is an upstream Skia design limitation exposed through SkiaSharp's SKCodec.Create and SKBitmap.Decode APIs.

codeInvestigation.finding: process_data() reads chunks in std::min(bufferSize=4096, length) increments. A 100MB chunk requires ~25,000 iterations through this loop, each calling png_process_data. No size guard, timeout, or cancellation point exists.

decodeBounds() loops through all pre-IDAT chunks. It only stops at IDAT. If a chunk before IDAT (e.g. eXIf or tEXt) is extremely large, the entire chunk must be read via process_data before reaching IDAT and actual image data.
sk_codec_new_from_stream calls SkCodec::MakeFromStream which invokes SkPngCodec::MakeFromStream → decodeBounds(). The C API provides no cancellation token or size limit parameter.
SKCodec.Create(SKStream) calls sk_codec_new_from_stream via P/Invoke. No timeout, cancellation, or maximum metadata size guard at the managed layer either.
SKBitmap.Decode(Stream) delegates to SKCodec.Create then Decode. The hang is in the Create step (decodeBounds) not the pixel decode step.

keySignals.text: my code flow is locked on SKBitmap.Decode(skCodec) call
keySignals.interpretation: The 'lock' is actually Skia reading the 100MB metadata chunk in 4KB increments during codec creation — extremely slow, not a deadlock.

keySignals.text: with other 100Mb pictures everything works fine so the huge Exif value seems to be the issue
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms the issue is specifically with pre-IDAT metadata size, not image pixel dimensions.

keySignals.text: In critical systems where Skia is used to process customer pictures, that issue can make the system down
keySignals.interpretation: Security/reliability concern — adversarial PNGs with large metadata could be used for DoS.

analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the decode path appears to hang on valid (though pathological) input with no way to cancel or bound execution time. This is an upstream Skia limitation in PNG chunk processing. Severity is medium because a workaround exists (pre-validate metadata size or strip Exif before decoding) and the trigger requires crafted input.
workarounds: Pre-validate PNG metadata size before passing to SkiaSharp — read the PNG chunks manually and reject files with excessively large pre-IDAT chunks
Strip or truncate Exif metadata using a library like MetadataExtractor or Magick.NET before decoding with SkiaSharp
Run SKBitmap.Decode on a background thread with a CancellationTokenSource and timeout, aborting the thread if it exceeds a threshold
nextQuestions: Would Skia upstream accept a maximum metadata chunk size limit or a cancellation mechanism in the codec?
Could SkiaSharp add a managed-side pre-scan of PNG chunks to reject pathological inputs before entering native code?
Is Thread.Abort or thread interruption reliable enough to terminate the blocked native call?

resolution.hypothesis: Skia's PNG codec reads all pre-IDAT chunks sequentially in 4KB buffers with no size limit. A ~100MB metadata chunk takes an extremely long time to process, appearing as a hang.

proposals.title: Pre-validate PNG metadata size
proposals.description: Before calling SKCodec.Create, read PNG chunk headers manually to check for oversized metadata chunks. Reject files exceeding a configurable threshold.

proposals.title: Strip Exif before decoding
proposals.description: Use a metadata library (MetadataExtractor, Magick.NET) to strip or truncate Exif data before passing the stream to SkiaSharp for decoding.

proposals.title: Add managed-side chunk size guard
proposals.description: Add an optional maximum metadata size parameter to SKCodec.Create that pre-scans PNG chunk headers and returns null if any pre-IDAT chunk exceeds the limit.

resolution.recommendedReason: Highest confidence workaround. The reporter already uses Magick.NET which can strip Exif. This avoids any SkiaSharp API changes and works immediately.

actionability.reason: Real bug with complete reproduction — Skia's PNG codec has no size guard for metadata chunks, causing apparent hangs on pathological input. Needs investigation into whether a managed-side guard or upstream Skia change is feasible.

actions.description: Apply bug and core SkiaSharp labels
Acknowledge the issue and provide workaround with explanation of root cause
Cross-reference PR #1518 which added Exif SubIFD support
